turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Reporting a Lost Mobile Phone

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, I’d like to report a lost phone.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course. When did you last see your phone?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I think it was this morning, just before I got on the train.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright. Was that at the station or on the train itself?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: At the station. Near the platform, I think.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay. Can you describe the phone for me?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It’s black with a dark blue case. The screen has a small crack on one corner.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thank you. Is it an Android phone or an iPhone?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It’s an iPhone. Sorry, I should have said that earlier.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: No problem at all. Have you tried calling the phone or using a tracking app?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I tried calling it, but it seems to be switched off now.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright. In that case, we can register it as lost. If someone finds it and hands it in, we’ll contact you.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. Do you need my phone number for that?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Actually, your email address would be best.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Oh, okay. I thought phone number first. I’ll give you my email.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great, thank you. I’d also recommend contacting your mobile provider to block the SIM card.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Block the SIM… you mean so no one can use it?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Exactly, to protect your personal information.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, I’ll do that today.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Is there anything else you’d like help with?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: No, that’s everything. Thank you.
